title: ⚔️ Chapter Boss ⭐⭐⭐: Word Formation: Prefixes and Suffixes

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "The ___ of the new policy has been questioned by several economists." (base word: EFFECTIVE — use the NEGATIVE noun form)
questions.explanation: The correct negative noun is ineffectiveness: in- (correct prefix for this adjective) + effective → ineffective → ineffectiveness. 'Uneffectiveness' uses the wrong prefix (effective takes in-, not un-). 'Diseffectiveness' is not a word. 'Ineffectivity' does exist but is rare and technical; ineffectiveness is the standard form.

options.text: Her misinterpretation of the data skewed the results.
The construction of the bridge took three years.
The government acted irresponsibly in this matter.
His behave during the meeting was unprofessional.

questions.explanation: Option (d) uses 'behave' (a verb) where a noun is needed: His behaviour during the meeting was unprofessional. Options (a), (b), and (c) all use correctly formed words: misinterpretation (noun), construction (noun), and irresponsibly (adverb).

questions.prompt: Complete: "Three of the bridge's support columns were found to be ___." (base word: STABLE — use the negative adjective)
questions.explanation: The correct negative form of stable is unstable (un- prefix). Although 'instable' exists in some technical contexts, 'unstable' is overwhelmingly the standard form. 'Misstable' and 'disstable' are not real words.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The new law makes it ___ to sell counterfeit goods." (base word: LEGAL — use the negative adjective)
questions.explanation: The prefix il- is used before roots starting with l: il- + legal = illegal. 'Unlegal' is not standard English. 'Inlegal' is not a word. 'Dislegal' is not a word. Illegal is the only correct form.

questions.prompt: Complete the formal sentence: "The scheme was praised for its ___, attracting investment from across the region." (base word: INNOVATE)
questions.explanation: The gap follows 'its' (a possessive determiner) and needs a noun: innovation (-tion suffix). 'Innovative' is an adjective ('its innovative' would need a following noun: 'its innovative approach'). 'Innovativeness' is a possible noun but far less common and natural than innovation here. 'Innovatively' is an adverb and cannot follow 'its'.

options.text: The complexity of the task was underestimated.
Her contribution was significant and widely recognised.
The proposal was meet with considerable resistance.
Irregular attendance led to poor performance.

questions.explanation: Option (c) uses 'meet' (base form) instead of the past participle 'met': The proposal was met with considerable resistance (passive construction). Options (a), (b), and (d) are all correct: complexity (noun), significantly (adverb), and irregular (negative adjective with ir-).
